skill.name: angular-migration
skill.description: Guides AngularJS to Angular migrations with hybrid mode, component rewrites, dependency injection updates, routing changes, forms migration, and staged rollout planning.
skill.summary: Plan and execute incremental AngularJS to Angular migrations with ngUpgrade guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown fences, relative API examples, and keyword matches in SKILL.md. Manual review found documentation-only Angular migration examples, no executable script content, no prompt injection, no secret access, and no external data exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Classified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external_commands findings point to Markdown code fence delimiters around TypeScript, JavaScript, HTML, and planning examples. These are documentation snippets in SKILL.md, not Ruby backtick execution. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.98. Confidence reasoning: The flagged lines are code block boundaries or surrounding sample code, and the file contains no executable Ruby or shell script invocation at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown code fences and examples, not executable command substitutions. The context in SKILL.md confirms instructional documentation only.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative API Examples Classified as Network Risk
low_findings.description: The network findings are AngularJS and Angular service examples using relative paths such as application API routes. They do not send data to an external host and do not collect secrets. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The examples are local application calls inside migration documentation, with no absolute URL, tracking endpoint, webhook, or credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations use relative application paths in illustrative service code. There is no evidence of exfiltration or unauthorized outbound network behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Keyword Matches Classified as Blocker Patterns
low_findings.description: The blocker findings are keyword matches in prose about Angular migration, dependencies, setup, resources, and testing. No weak cryptographic algorithm, system reconnaissance command, prompt injection text, or malicious instruction was found. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: Review of the cited SKILL.md regions found migration guidance and resource names only, with no sensitive file access, environment collection, command execution, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain migration prose and resource references. No cryptographic function or reconnaissance behavior appears in the reviewed context.

content.value_statement: Legacy AngularJS migrations are risky when teams change too much at once. This skill guides incremental Angular adoption with hybrid mode, component conversion, and rollout planning.
actual_capabilities: Compares big bang, hybrid, and vertical slice migration strategies.
Shows hybrid AngularJS and Angular bootstrap setup with ngUpgrade.
Maps AngularJS controllers, directives, services, routes, and forms to Angular patterns.
Explains dependency injection bridging between AngularJS and Angular services.
Provides a phased migration timeline for planning setup, infrastructure, feature work, and cleanup.
Lists practical pitfalls around change detection, scope handling, and test coverage.
limitations: Does not include runnable scripts or generated migration tooling.
Does not inspect a repository automatically or produce a complete migration estimate.
Uses simplified examples that must be adapted to each application architecture.
Does not cover every Angular version change after the initial Angular migration.

use_cases.title: Plan a Legacy Migration
use_cases.description: Choose a migration strategy, define milestones, and reduce delivery risk for an AngularJS application.

use_cases.title: Convert Application Code
use_cases.description: Translate AngularJS controllers, directives, services, routes, and forms into Angular equivalents.

use_cases.title: Review Hybrid Architecture
use_cases.description: Validate ngUpgrade setup, dependency injection bridges, and incremental rollout boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Migration Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this AngularJS application structure and recommend whether we should use hybrid migration, vertical slice migration, or a full rewrite.

prompt_templates.title: Create Migration Milestones
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a phased AngularJS to Angular migration plan for our app. Include setup, services, routing, feature migration, testing, and cleanup milestones.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this AngularJS controller, directive, and service into Angular components and services. Explain the dependency injection changes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Hybrid Interop
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this hybrid AngularJS and Angular design. Identify ngUpgrade risks, service downgrade issues, routing conflicts, and testing gaps.

output_examples.input: A team needs to move a large AngularJS app to Angular without pausing feature delivery.
output: Phased timeline for setup, shared services, feature slices, and cleanup.
Key risks for routing, dependency injection, and test coverage.

output_examples.input: A developer provides an AngularJS directive and asks how to migrate it.
output: Angular component structure with inputs and outputs described in prose.
Notes on replacing scope bindings and directive templates.
Testing and rollout checks for the converted component.

output_examples.input: A technical lead wants to validate a hybrid ngUpgrade plan.
output: Checklist for bootstrap, module boundaries, service bridging, and route ownership.
Recommended migration order for low-risk incremental delivery.
Warnings about mixed patterns and change detection differences.

best_practices: Start with services and shared utilities before migrating complex user interface flows.
Keep hybrid compatibility tests active until AngularJS code is fully removed.
Document migration decisions, rollback points, and ownership for each feature slice.
anti_patterns: Do not start with a big bang rewrite unless the application is small and well understood.
Do not mix AngularJS and Angular patterns inside one feature without clear boundaries.
Do not remove AngularJS infrastructure before migrated routes and services are validated.

faq.question: Does this skill run migration commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides migration guidance and examples, but it does not include runnable scripts or execute commands.

faq.question: Can it support incremental AngularJS migration?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on hybrid mode, ngUpgrade, and feature-by-feature migration planning.

faq.question: Does it cover dependency injection changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains upgrading AngularJS services and downgrading Angular services for hybrid applications.

faq.question: Can it migrate routing?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes moving AngularJS routes to Angular Router patterns and highlights route ownership concerns.

faq.question: Is it enough for a complete enterprise migration?
faq.answer: No. Large migrations still need repository analysis, test planning, stakeholder review, and phased delivery management.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is packaged for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
